properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the King River at Docker Road Bridge.

Minor flooding likely at Docker Road bridge from Tuesday afternoon. 

Rainfall across the Ovens and King River catchments since Saturday afternoon has resulted in renewed river level rises across the catchments. Minor flooding is likely to develop along the King River at Docker Road Bridge from Tuesday afternoon.

King River:
Minor flooding is likely along the King River. The King River at Docker Rd Bridge is currently at 3.63 m and rising, below the minor flood level. The King River at Docker Rd Bridge is likely to exceed the minor flood level (3.70 m) from Tuesday afternoon. The river may reach around 3.80 m overnight Tuesday into Wednesday, with minor flooding.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings. 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 15/07/2026 12:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Kiewa River Downstream of Mongans Bridge
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for Kiewa River Downstream of Mongans Bridge.

Minor flooding is continuing at Bandiana.

The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is currently at 3.00 m and falling, below the minor flood level. The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is likely to remain below the minor flood level (3.30 m) over the next few days.

The Kiewa River at Bandiana is currently at 2.89 m and steady, with minor flooding. The Kiewa River at Bandiana is expected to remain above the minor flood level (2.80 m) over the next few days.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 15/07/2026 12:30 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
FLOODING NO LONGER EXPECTED AT BARHAM

Flooding is no longer expected at Barham.

Murray River downstream of Tocumwal to Barham:

The Murray River at Barham peaked at 5.38 m around 10:45 pm Monday, below the minor flood level (5.50 m). The river is currently at 5.37 m and steady, below the minor flood level.The Murray River at Barham is expected to remain below the minor flood level (5.50 m) for the remainder of the week.

Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.

Safety Advice: 
  • Don't drive, walk, swim or play in floodwater because it is dangerous. 
  • Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways. 
  • Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads. 
  • Check the ABC and local media for updates. The situation can change quickly, so stay informed.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Boating in floodwaters is dangerous due to debris, submerged trees and other hidden dangers. 

This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

This is a final warning. No further warnings will be issued for this event.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Seven Creeks.

Minor flooding occurring at Euroa.

Rainfall across the Seven and Castle Creeks catchments since Saturday evening has resulted in renewed river level rises across the catchments. 

Seven Creeks to Euroa:

The river level is around the minor flood level at Euroa.

The Seven Creeks at Euroa is currently at 2.48 m and steady, around the minor flood level. The Seven Creeks at Euroa may remain around the minor flood level (2.50 m) until Tuesday evening. The creek may fall below the minor flood level (2.50 m) overnight Tuesday into Wednesday.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 15/07/2026 01:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
